marketFocus: RBA Hikes to 4.10% — Hawkish Bullock Drives AUD Recovery: RBA delivered the widely expected back-to-back 25bps hike (5-4 vote split) to 4.10%. AUD initially sold off on the narrow majority, but Governor Bullock's press conference was decisively hawkish — she stated oil prices were NOT the reason for the hike, inflation was already too high, and the dissenters voted to hold in a hawkish sense (debate was about timing, not direction). AUD/USD rebounded from the initial dip and is firming into the European open. RBA Employment Report Thursday remains the next key AUD catalyst.

Iran War: Oil Partially Recovers as Iran Denies US Contact — Hormuz Remains Closed: WTI settled $5.21 lower at $93.50/bbl Monday (Brent $100.21/bbl) as Trump suggested Iran wants to make a deal. However, oil rebounded overnight after Iran denied contact with the US, and Iranian attacks on neighbouring energy infrastructure (Fujairah fire, Abu Dhabi Shah gas field suspended, Iraq Majnoon oilfield hit by drones) reinforced supply disruption fears. Fujairah port suspended oil loadings. BofA raised Brent forecast to $77.50/bbl for 2026 (prior $61/bbl); Morgan Stanley raised Q2-2026 Brent to $110/bbl. Risk environment remains Mixed — geopolitical premium intact but de-escalation hopes cap the upside.

Super Week of Central Banks: FOMC (Wed), BOC (Wed), ECB (Thu), BOE (Thu), SNB (Thu), BOJ (Thu): Today's key data: German/EZ ZEW Economic Sentiment (Mar), US Retail Sales m/m. DXY regained composure after Monday's softness, trading back near 100.00. EUR/USD slipped back below 1.1500 as dollar firmed. GBP/USD retested 1.3300 support. USD/JPY rebounded from sub-159.00 after Finance Minister Katayama reiterated readiness for decisive FX steps. BoJ Governor Ueda confirmed price trend rising gradually — no near-term hike signal.

USD.tone: Haven + Fed On Hold + DXY Regaining Composure
USD.summary: DXY regained composure after Monday's softness, trading back near 100.00 as oil partially recovered and Trump's Iran deal optimism faded. The structural bullish case is intact: Core PCE 3.1% YoY keeps the Fed firmly on hold, money markets price only 18bps of easing for the full year, and the geopolitical premium from the Hormuz crisis supports safe-haven flows. US Retail Sales m/m today at 13:30 FFT is the first test — a strong print would reinforce the USD bid ahead of Wednesday's FOMC + PPI. Trump reiterated criticism of Fed Chair Powell and called for a rate cut, but markets are ignoring political pressure. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: DXY regaining composure near 100.00 after Monday's softness — structural bid intact
Core PCE 3.1% YoY (above 3.0% exp) — hot inflation keeps Fed firmly on hold
Money markets: only 18bps of Fed easing priced for full year 2026
Geopolitical safe-haven premium: Hormuz crisis, oil supply disruption ongoing
US Retail Sales m/m (TUE 13:30 FFT) + FOMC + PPI (WED) are the key tests

EUR.summary: EUR/USD slipped back below 1.1500 as the dollar regained composure, confirming the single currency cannot hold any rally in this environment. Europe's energy import dependence remains the dominant narrative — Brent above $100/bbl acts as a direct tax on the Eurozone economy. German/EZ ZEW Economic Sentiment today will be the first hard data point reflecting the Iran war shock. Bund futures were rangebound, capped heading into ZEW data. The ECB meets Thursday with updated macro projections — markets now fully price a 25bps ECB hike by year-end (was cuts two weeks ago). Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: EUR/USD slipped back below 1.1500 — dollar regained composure, rally capped
Energy importer: Brent $100+ acts as direct tax on Eurozone economy
German/EZ ZEW Economic Sentiment (TUE 11:00 FFT) — first hard data of Iran war shock
ECB repricing: markets now fully price 25bps ECB hike by year-end (was cuts)
ECB (THU 14:15 FFT) + updated macro projections = key catalyst

GBP.tone: Stagflation Trap + 1.3300 Support Test + BOE Thursday
GBP.summary: GBP/USD partially faded Monday's gains and retested the 1.3300 level to the downside, finding only slight support. The structural bearish case is unchanged: flat January GDP (0.0%), the BoE in a policy straitjacket, and the energy shock adding to stagflation pressure. UK PM Starmer announced a £50mln household support package and a fuel price cap until September — policy response to the energy shock, but not a bullish catalyst for GBP. Trump stated he is not happy with the UK but thinks they will be involved in the Hormuz coalition. UK Jobs Report (Wednesday 08:00 FFT) and BOE Meeting (Thursday 13:00 FFT) are the key catalysts. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: GBP/USD retested 1.3300 support — slight support found but structural bearish intact
Stagflation trap: GDP 0.0% MoM in January + energy shock adding to inflation
UK PM Starmer: £50mln household support + fuel price cap until September
Trump: 'not happy with UK' but expects UK involvement in Hormuz coalition
UK Jobs Report (WED 08:00 FFT) + BOE Meeting (THU 13:00 FFT) = key catalysts

JPY.tone: Energy Importer + Verbal Intervention Only + BOJ Thursday
JPY.summary: USD/JPY rebounded from a brief dip to sub-159.00 territory as the dollar and oil partially recovered. Finance Minister Katayama reiterated readiness to take decisive FX steps if needed — but verbal warnings continue to be largely ignored by the market. BoJ Governor Ueda confirmed the price trend is rising gradually and underlying inflation is accelerating toward the 2% target, but reaffirmed the BoJ will act flexibly in exceptional situations when JGB yields suddenly rise — no near-term hike signal. Japan's 20-year JGB auction was mixed (higher bid-to-cover but lower accepted prices). The BOJ meets Thursday and is widely expected to hold at 0.75%. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: USD/JPY rebounded from sub-159.00 — Finance Minister Katayama verbal warnings ignored
BoJ Governor Ueda: price trend rising gradually, no near-term hike signal
Japan 20yr JGB auction mixed — higher b/c 3.25x (prev. 3.08) but lower accepted prices
Japan imports 90%+ of crude from Middle East — Hormuz closure = direct terms-of-trade hit
BOJ (THU ~04:00 FFT) expected to hold at 0.75% — 160.00 remains line in sand

AUD.tone: RBA Hiked to 4.10% + Hawkish Bullock + Energy Exporter
AUD.summary: AUD/USD initially sold off on the narrow 5-4 RBA vote split but recovered sharply as Governor Bullock delivered a hawkish press conference — she explicitly stated oil prices were NOT the reason for the hike, inflation was already too high, and the dissenters voted to hold in a hawkish sense (debate was about timing, not direction). This is a strong bullish signal: the RBA is hiking into an energy shock, not despite it. Australia is a net energy exporter, benefiting from higher oil prices. ASX 200 was supported by gains in miners, utilities, and financials. China's Ministry of Finance confirmed more proactive fiscal policy — positive for AUD via the China demand channel. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: RBA hiked to 4.10% (5-4 vote) — Bullock hawkish: 'oil not the reason, inflation too high'
AUD/USD initial dip on narrow vote recovered — hawkish press conference drove rebound
Dissenters voted to hold in hawkish sense — debate was about timing, not direction
Australia is net energy exporter — benefits from higher oil prices (unlike EUR/JPY)
China MoF: more proactive fiscal policy in 2026 — AUD positive via demand channel

CAD.tone: Petro-Currency + IEA Supply Role + BOC Wednesday
CAD.summary: CAD remains supported by WTI/Brent near $100/bbl and Canada's confirmed role supplying 24mln bbls as part of the IEA stabilisation effort. Canadian CPI for February was released yesterday — the result reinforces the BOC's inflation constraint from triple-digit oil. USD/CAD is trading near the 50-day MA at 1.3700 after last week's employment shock (-83.9k). The BOC meets Wednesday at 14:45 FFT — the key question is whether the energy windfall or the labor market deterioration wins the narrative. BofA and Morgan Stanley both raised oil price forecasts significantly, which is structurally positive for CAD. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: WTI $93.50/bbl (settled) — Brent $100.21/bbl; petro-currency trade intact
Canada supplying 24mln bbls oil as part of IEA stabilisation effort
BofA raised Brent forecast to $77.50/bbl 2026 (prev. $61/bbl); MS raised Q2 to $110/bbl
Employment shock last week -83.9k (vs +10k exp) — downside risk to CAD remains
BOC Meeting (WED 14:45 FFT) = key directional catalyst for CAD

CHF.tone: Haven vs Energy Importer + SNB Thursday
CHF.summary: CHF remains in a tug-of-war between haven demand from the Hormuz crisis and Switzerland's energy import dependence. Swiss sight deposits rose to CHF 454.4bln (prev. CHF 454.07bln) — marginal increase, no aggressive SNB intervention signal. EUR/CHF stable as both currencies are treated as energy-short assets. Spot gold edged higher after rebounding from a brief dip beneath $5,000/oz — correlated haven asset. The SNB meets Thursday at 09:30 FFT — Chairman Schlegel's tone on imported inflation vs currency strength will be the key signal. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Swiss sight deposits CHF 454.4bln (prev. 454.07bln) — no aggressive SNB intervention
EUR/CHF stable — both currencies treated as energy-short assets
Haven demand from Hormuz crisis — but Switzerland is net energy importer
Gold edged higher after brief dip below $5,000/oz — correlated haven asset
SNB Meeting (THU 09:30 FFT) — Schlegel tone on inflation vs CHF strength = key signal

NZD.tone: High-Beta Risk Proxy + No RBNZ + AUD Outperforming
NZD.summary: NZD/USD was mixed overnight after Monday's outperformance, with Antipodeans diverging as AUD benefited from the hawkish RBA while NZD lacked a domestic catalyst. The Hang Seng outperformed but the mainland Shanghai Comp lagged after Trump revealed the US asked to delay the Trump-Xi summit by a month due to the Iran war — a negative signal for NZD via the China demand channel. US-China trade talks in Paris were described as constructive but the summit delay caps the upside. No RBNZ meeting until April. NZD GDP Q4 (Wednesday 22:45 FFT) is the only domestic release this week. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: NZD/USD mixed overnight — AUD outperforming on hawkish RBA, NZD lacks catalyst
Trump-Xi summit delayed by a month due to Iran war — negative for NZD via China channel
Hang Seng outperformed but Shanghai Comp lagged — mixed China signal
No RBNZ meeting until April — policy support absent
NZD GDP Q4 (WED 22:45 FFT) — only domestic catalyst this week
